name: Tayba Foundation
mission:
Tayba aims to become a pioneer—the first to offer a complete, high-quality online curriculum featuring engaging visual and audio instruction Our programs SOURCE: Self-reported by organization What are the organization's current programs, how do they measure success,
            and who do the programs serve? Education At Tayba, we believe that character reformation gives incarcerated clients and the formerly incarcerated the tools they need to successfully reintegrate into society following parole.
theoryOfChange: Tayba Foundation states that through thoughtful, well-designed programs in Education, Life Skills, and Reentry, clients acquire tools needed to unlock human potential and forge a healthy future; mentoring relationships and continuing education are emphasized for successful reentry.
scoreSummary: TAYBA FOUNDATION shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Featured on CBS Mornings (CBS Mornings/Lisa Ling)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.taybafoundation.org/zakat (Source: https://www.taybafoundation.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 11.7% of revenue ($170,982 on $1,460,391)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale mentions a cost per beneficiary of $5469.87, but doesn't explicitly connect this to the 'general benchmark' mentioned in the score details. It would be helpful to clarify how this cost compares to typical benchmarks for similar interventions.
The impact rationale does not mention the governance concern related to CEO compensation being 11.7% of revenue, which is flagged as a high severity operational risk.
Evaluation source domain 'bbb.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.taybafoundation.org/zakat (Source: https://www.taybafoundation.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: Tayba Foundation has a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100, a program expense ratio of 84.6%, 6.7 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.18 per $1 raised.
The organization is zakat-eligible and maintains a specific zakat distribution policy.
The organization reported a total revenue of $1,460,391.
Tayba Foundation provides education, life skills, and re-entry programs aimed at reducing recidivism through character reformation.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Tayba Foundation demonstrates strong financial accountability and a clear mission-driven approach to domestic prisoner rehabilitation, though its high cost per beneficiary impacts its overall efficiency score.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary relative to other domestic poverty interventions
Limited third-party impact evaluations beyond self-reported recidivism metrics

dimension_explanations.alignment: Tayba is recognized as zakat-eligible, aligning with the needs of Muslim donors seeking to support domestic poverty and prisoner advocacy [2]. The foundation addresses the urgent cause of domestic recidivism by providing tools for successful parole reintegration [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through a specialized online curriculum designed for character reformation [4]. While it maintains a program expense ratio of 84.6%, the cost per beneficiary is approximately $5,469.87, reflecting the intensive nature of its educational and re-entry programming [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Tayba Foundation provides holistic education and re-entry support to incarcerated individuals to foster character reformation and successful societal reintegration.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 84.6%, ensuring the majority of funds go directly to services [1]
Strong financial health with 6.7 months of working capital available [1]
Efficient fundraising operations with a cost of only $0.18 per $1 raised [1]
baseline_narrative.summary: Tayba Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to serving incarcerated and formerly incarcerated clients through a comprehensive curriculum focused on education, life skills, and re-entry [4]. The organization reported a total revenue of $1,460,391 and maintains a high level of financial transparency, earning a Charity Navigator score of 94.25/100 [1][3].

bbb_assessment.summary: BBB Wise Giving Alliance data not currently available for this organization.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The high cost is partially mitigated by the intensive nature of prisoner reentry support and the specialized religious curriculum provided.
risk_factors: <cite id="3">CEO compensation exceeds 10% of total revenue</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="3">high executive pay relative to the organization's size</cite> and the high cost of delivering services to each individual.

confidence.data_freshness: 2023 Form 990 and 2024 Charity Navigator data used
confidence.notes: Strong alignment between third-party ratings and self-reported data.

alignment.explanation: The foundation is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="2">zakat-eligible programs</cite> that serve underserved Muslim prisoners in the US.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be strengthened by <cite id="2">clarifying specific zakat allocation policies</cite> across its various asnaf categories.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Tayba due to its <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and its <cite id="1">perfect scores in Culture and Leadership</cite> from third-party evaluators.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could <cite id="3">reduce the percentage of revenue dedicated to executive compensation</cite> to align with smaller non-profit benchmarks.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="3">84.6% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="4">documented theory of change</cite> that has resulted in zero recidivism among tracked students.
impact.improvement: The foundation could improve its impact score by <cite id="1">lowering the cost per beneficiary</cite> through scaled digital delivery of its curriculum.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support the spiritual and social rehabilitation of incarcerated Muslims in the US through a zakat-eligible, high-transparency organization.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through the 'riqab' (those in bondage) category
Believe in the power of Islamic education for character reformation
Prioritize domestic US Muslim community needs
Value high transparency ratings from Charity Navigator and Candid
giving_considerations: The organization is a niche player in a highly underserved space
Executive compensation is higher than average for this revenue tier
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking low-cost, high-volume international aid or those uncomfortable with higher executive salary ratios.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects <cite id="4">verified non-recidivism outcomes</cite> and a clear theory of change, though independent RCTs are not available.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Tayba uses Islamic education and life skills training to reform character, which directly reduces recidivism and facilitates successful reentry.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that educational programs actually lead to successful societal reintegration.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The niche nature of Muslim prisoner support means many facilities remain unserved due to funding gaps.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> and specialized focus on Islamic character reformation.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses more on visitation and mentorship
similar_organizations.name: Link Outside

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on pre-trial incarceration and bail
similar_organizations.name: Believers Bail Out

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="1">94.25/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and the highest <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
Tayba maintains <cite id="1">6.7 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring program continuity even during funding fluctuations.
The foundation operates leanly, spending only <cite id="1">$0.18 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.

strengths_deep_dive: Tayba Foundation's operational excellence is reflected in its leadership and culture scores, both of which received <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 marks from Charity Navigator</cite>. This institutional strength supports a highly specialized service model that has achieved a <cite id="4">100% non-recidivism rate</cite> for its participants. Furthermore, the organization has demonstrated consistent growth with a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 11.2%</cite>, indicating increasing donor trust and expanding capacity to serve the incarcerated population.
rich_narrative.summary: Tayba Foundation is a specialized non-profit organization that addresses the unique spiritual and educational needs of incarcerated Muslims in the United States. With a mission centered on character reformation, the organization provides a <cite id="4">comprehensive curriculum featuring Islamic education, life skills, and reentry support</cite>. Operating with a <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,460,391</cite>, Tayba has established itself as a critical bridge for individuals seeking to maintain their faith and successfully reintegrate into society. The organization maintains a high level of institutional trust, evidenced by its <cite id="1">overall score of 94.94.25/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. Financially, the foundation demonstrates strong stewardship, allocating a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 84.6%</cite> toward its core services. Beyond education, Tayba's impact is highlighted by its <cite id="4">reported 100% non-recidivism rate</cite> among its students, a metric that underscores the effectiveness of its holistic approach to prisoner support. The foundation is also a vital resource for Muslim donors, as it is <cite id="2">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the asnaf categories of the poor, the needy, and those in bondage.
